results.title: Ballet in New Zealand
results.creator_credit: Robert Lapresle, Cyril Morton, Geoffrey Scott and New Zealand National Film Unit
results.brief_description: The standard of ballet in New Zealand is shown in excerpts from four works - ‘Napoli’, ‘Do-wack-a-do’, ‘Les Sylphides’, ‘Prismatic variations’. A good representative selection which shows competence, imagination and enthusiasm.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Robert Lapresle, Cyril Morton, Geoffrey Scott and New Zealand National Film Unit

results.title: Ballet girl
results.brief_description: This film follows a small girl at the Royal Danish Ballet School. Follows her awakening interest in ballet, her entrance test, ballet classes and school lessons, her admiration of the older students and her progress to her first school ballet concert.
results.description: This film follows a small girl at the Royal Danish Ballet School. Follows her awakening interest in ballet, her entrance test, ballet classes and school lessons, her admiration of the older students and her progress to her first school ballet concert.

results.title: Ballet adagio
results.creator_credit: Norman McLaren and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: A slow motion study by Norman McLaren of the pas-de-deux adagio, one of the most exacting and difficult dances of the classical ballet. A ballet originally choreographed by the Russian ballet master Asaf Messerer is performed in this film by the internationally known Canadian pair David and Anna Marie Holmes, to the music of Albinoni’s “Adagio”. A film to heighten the aesthetic appreciation of classical ballet and to afford observation of the technique and mechanics of the adagio movements. Photographed by Jaques Fogel and Douglas Kiefer. (Awards: Melbourne, Atlanta, Columbus, Bogota)
results.creator_credit_for_label: Norman McLaren and National Film Board of Canada

results.title: Ballads of Bullant Ridge. No. 2
results.creator_credit: Alan Marshall, Ronald Mason and Academy Film Productions
results.brief_description: Alan Marshall stories set to traditional Australian bush music and illustrated by cartoonist Douglas Trainsh. The items featured are your nose’ and ‘The Ghost of Captain Candlelight’.

results.title: Ballads of Bullant Ridge. No. 1
results.creator_credit: Alan Marshall, Chris Gallagher, Ronald Mason and Academy Film Productions
results.brief_description: A series of bush ballad songs based on the “Speewa Jack” characters from the Alan Marshall stories illustrated by cartoonist Douglas Trainsh. The songs are ‘The Strongest man in town’; ‘The Boxing kangaroo’; and ‘Goanna oil’.

results.title: Balinese interlude
results.brief_description: A travelogue on Bali, one of the 7,000 islands that make up the land mass of Indonesia. We visit a fishing village, see examples and areas showing the ingenious terrace cultivation of rice. Many of the entertainments shown are based on Hindu-Balinese religious beliefs which also play a large part in the everyday life of villagers.

results.title: Bali: the release of a soul
results.creator_credit: John Phillips, Ted Myers and United Cinema
results.brief_description: To the Balinese, life and religion are one. The film explains this as it explains some aspects of the school system, religion and village life, to the accompaniment of gamelan music.

results.title: The Balance sheet barrier: business finance for non-specialists (NP)
results.brief_description: Defines the business finance terms of share capital, loan capital, reserves, fixed assets, working capital, profit, overheads and cash flow. Emphasises that the speed at which working capital turns around is central to the profitability of a business. Illustrates that the balance sheet, the profit and loss account and the cash flow forecast are expressions of the present, past and future. Intended for management, induction training for staff recruited to financial departments and for senior schools. Starring John Cleese and Ronnie Corbett.

results.title: Balance 1950
results.brief_description: Animated drawings showing the balance sheet of Imperial Chemical Industries for 1950. Vivacious little “match stick” men, aided by ever active “Pound” and other devices perform against a backdrop of graphs and ledgers. An interesting example of simplified animation technique in which an unpopular subject is treated in an entertaining way.

results.title: Balablok
results.creator_credit: Brent Walker Film Productions, Rene Jodoin and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: An animated replay of the human comedy as amusing in its perception as in the way it caricatures man’s propensity to resort to violence rather than reason. The whole area of conflict is reduced to its simplest dimensions - cubes and balls representing opposing protagonists. Why they fight and what happens after the battle is a parody of human nature. A film without commentary. (Awards: Palme d’Or, Cannes; Colombo; Buenos Aires)
